title: Maharashtra Heat Wave: Intense Heat Hits the State; 17 Heatstroke Patients Found in One and a Half Months - Lokshahi English News
full_text:
Intense Heat Hits the State
Maharashtra Heat Wave: The intensity of the heat is being felt more acutely, causing distress among citizens. Going outside during the afternoon has become difficult, and there has been a significant increase in temperature in the current weather. As a result of the rise in temperature, the effects of the heat are increasing.
In the afternoon, the sun feels more intense. There is a possibility of further temperature increases in some areas. The impact of the heat has also increased in rural areas. In some places, the discomfort from the heat has been observed to be rising, leading to an increase in illnesses. The harshness of the heat is growing day by day. Against this backdrop, it has been reported that 17 heatstroke patients have been found in the state over one and a half months. Ratnagiri district has recorded the highest number of four patients, while Pune district has reported three patients. Two patients each have been recorded in Jalna and Palghar.
Additionally, there has been one patient each reported in Gadchiroli, Nashik, Raigad, Gondia, Jalgaon, and Nandurbar. The highest number of patients has been found in Ratnagiri and Pune districts, and the health department has clarified that there have been no deaths among the patients so far.
Short Summary
Intensity of heat has increased in the state
17 heatstroke patients in one and a half months
4 patients reported in Ratnagiri
3 patients recorded in Pune
1-2 patients in other districts
No deaths reported so far
Clarification from the health department
